deep_dive.title: OpenThoughts-Agent: Data Recipes for Agentic Models
deep_dive.abstract: Agentic language models dramatically expand the applications of AI yet little is publicly known about how to curate training data for broadly capable agents. Existing open efforts such as SWE-Smith, SERA, and Nemotron-Terminal typically target a single benchmark, leaving open the question of how to train models that generalize across diverse agentic tasks. The OpenThoughts-Agent (OT-Agent) project addresses this gap with a fully open data curation pipeline for training agentic models. We conduct more than 100 controlled ablation experiments to systematically investigate each stage of the pipeline, yielding insights on the importance of task sources and diversity. We then assemble a training set of 100K examples from our pipeline and fine-tune Qwen3-32B on this dataset, which yields an average accuracy of 44.8% across seven agentic benchmarks and a 3.9 percentage point improvement over the strongest existing open data agentic model (Nemotron-Terminal-32B, 40.9%). Moreover, our training data exhibits strong scaling properties, outperforming alternative open datasets at every training set size in compute-controlled comparisons. We publicly release our training sets, data pipeline, experimental data, and models at openthoughts.ai to support future open research on agentic model training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Open data curation pipeline for training agentic models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于训练智能体模型的开源数据策划流程
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于训练智能体模型的开源数据策划流程
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于训练智能体模型的开源数据策划流程
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: OpenThoughts-Agent provides a fully open data curation pipeline for training agentic LLMs, with 100+ ablation experiments revealing the importance of task diversity, and fine-tunes Qwen3-32B to strong generalization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对如何为通用智能体策划训练数据这一开放问题，OpenThoughts-Agent项目提出完全开源的数据策划流水线，通过100多项控制消融实验系统研究各阶段影响，最终汇编10万条多任务示例并微调Qwen3-32B，在多个智能体基准测试上展现了强大的泛化能力。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对如何为通用智能体策划训练数据这一开放问题，OpenThoughts-Agent项目提出完全开源的数据策划流水线，通过100多项控制消融实验系统研究各阶段影响，最终汇编10万条多任务示例并微调Qwen3-32B，在多个智能体基准测试上展现了强大的泛化能力。

deep_dive.title: The Interplay of Harness Design and Post-Training in LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Tool-integrated LLM agents are often wrapped within a harness: the scaffolding that determines which tools are exposed, how they are described, and what auxiliary information accompanies each per-step observation. While agents are routinely post-trained, this scaffolding is typically treated as a fixed engineering detail, with design effort limited to the training-free regime. Moreover, existing post-training algorithms assume a static environment, even though tool environments and tasks often shift upon deployment. To address this gap, we extend $\texttt{ALFWorld}$ (i) to treat the harness as a controllable design dimension and (ii) to support evaluation under task and tool environment shifts. Building on this, we systematically analyze how the harness design influences post-training in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution (OOD) settings. We empirically show that harness-aware post-training not only improves in-distribution performance but also enables agents to robustly adapt to OOD settings. Under a harness with minimal design effort, post-training suffers a drastic performance drop under stronger tool environment shifts, further highlighting the importance of harness-aware post-training under such shifts.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Studies interaction of harness design and post-training for LLM agents
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究脚手架设计与LLM智能体后训练的交互
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究脚手架设计与LLM智能体后训练的交互
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究脚手架设计与LLM智能体后训练的交互
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Extends ALFWorld to study how harness design (tool scaffolding) affects post-trained LLM agent robustness under task and tool shifts, revealing key design principles for adaptable agents.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文指出LLM智能体通常被固定在工具脚手架中，而后训练算法假设静态环境，导致部署后应对任务和工具环境漂移的能力不足。为此，扩展ALFWorld将脚手架作为可控设计维度并支持环境漂移评估，系统分析脚手架设计如何影响后训练智能体的泛化性，并提出使智能体更加鲁棒的设计原则。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文指出LLM智能体通常被固定在工具脚手架中，而后训练算法假设静态环境，导致部署后应对任务和工具环境漂移的能力不足。为此，扩展ALFWorld将脚手架作为可控设计维度并支持环境漂移评估，系统分析脚手架设计如何影响后训练智能体的泛化性，并提出使智能体更加鲁棒的设计原则。

deep_dive.title: Neglected Free Lunch from Post-training: Progress Advantage for LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Process reward models enable fine-grained, step-level evaluation of LLMs, yet building them for agentic settings remains prohibitively difficult: long-horizon interactions, irreversible actions, and stochastic environment feedback make both human annotation and Monte Carlo estimation infeasible at scale. In this work, we show that reinforcement learning (RL) post-training already provides the ingredients for effective step-level scoring, eliminating the need for dedicated reward model training altogether. Concretely, we derive an implicit advantage under a general stochastic Markov decision process, which we term progress advantage -- log-probability ratio between the RL-trained policy and its reference policy exactly recovers the optimal advantage function. This formulation makes the resulting signal annotation-free, domain-agnostic, and available as a byproduct of the standard RL post-training pipeline. We validate the effectiveness of the progress advantage across three different applications: test-time scaling, uncertainty quantification, and failure attribution on five benchmarks and four model families. Across all settings, it consistently outperforms confidence-based baselines and, despite requiring no task-specific training, surpasses dedicated trained reward models. We complement these results with deeper analyses on characteristics of progress advantage, offering practical guidance for adoption in real-world agentic systems.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Derives progress advantage from RL post-training for step-level scoring of LLM agents, eliminating separate reward model training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 从RL后训练中推导出进度优势用于LLM智能体的步骤级评分，无需单独训练奖励模型。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 从RL后训练中推导出进度优势用于LLM智能体的步骤级评分，无需单独训练奖励模型。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 从RL后训练中推导出进度优势用于LLM智能体的步骤级评分，无需单独训练奖励模型。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper shows that RL post-training provides an implicit progress advantage for LLM agents that can serve as step-level reward, making dedicated process reward model training unnecessary, and verifies effectiveness on agent benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对构建LLM智能体步骤级过程奖励模型的困难（长程交互、不可逆动作、随机反馈），提出利用RL后训练中已有的策略与参考策略的对数概率比作为隐式进度优势，可替代显式奖励模型进行步骤级评分。在通用随机MDP下推导出该优势的最优性，并在LLM智能体基准上验证了其有效性，无需额外训练即可获得细粒度评估信号，实现了后训练的免费收益。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对构建LLM智能体步骤级过程奖励模型的困难（长程交互、不可逆动作、随机反馈），提出利用RL后训练中已有的策略与参考策略的对数概率比作为隐式进度优势，可替代显式奖励模型进行步骤级评分。在通用随机MDP下推导出该优势的最优性，并在LLM智能体基准上验证了其有效性，无需额外训练即可获得细粒度评估信号，实现了后训练的免费收益。

deep_dive.title: Semantic Consistency Policy Optimization for Reinforcement Learning of LLM Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Group-based reinforcement learning effectively post-trains LLM agents for long-horizon, sparse-reward tasks by deriving step-level credit from trajectory outcomes. However, this ties a step's credit to its rollout's final outcome: semantically near-identical intermediate steps receive opposite credit depending on whether their trajectory eventually succeeded or failed. Such semantic credit inconsistency sends conflicting gradients to similar actions and wastes the partially-correct progress inside failed rollouts. Motivated by this, we propose Semantic Consistency Policy Optimization (SCPO), a value-free reward-shaping method that mitigates this inconsistency by recovering step-level credit from successful siblings in the same rollout group. Concretely, SCPO scores each failed step against a successful sibling and adds positive step-level credit for new progress along that sibling. On ALFWorld and WebShop, SCPO matches or exceeds strong group-based baselines, reaching 93.7+/-4.1 percent success on ALFWorld and 74.8+/-2.0 percent on WebShop at 1.5B parameters, with gains concentrated on the hardest multi-step tasks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: RL-based post-training of LLM agents with semantic credit consistency
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于RL的LLM智能体后训练，解决语义信用不一致
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于RL的LLM智能体后训练，解决语义信用不一致
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于RL的LLM智能体后训练，解决语义信用不一致
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Addresses credit inconsistency in group-based RL for LLM agents by recovering step-level credit from successful siblings, improving post-training efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对群组强化学习中因轨迹结局不同导致语义相似中间步骤获得相反信用的问题，提出语义一致性策略优化方法，通过从同组成功轨迹中恢复步骤级信用，缓解信用不一致，实验表明该方法使LLM智能体在长时程稀疏奖励任务中的后训练更加有效，充分利用了失败轨迹中的部分正确进展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对群组强化学习中因轨迹结局不同导致语义相似中间步骤获得相反信用的问题，提出语义一致性策略优化方法，通过从同组成功轨迹中恢复步骤级信用，缓解信用不一致，实验表明该方法使LLM智能体在长时程稀疏奖励任务中的后训练更加有效，充分利用了失败轨迹中的部分正确进展。

deep_dive.title: Offline Multi-agent Continual Cooperation via Skill Partition and Reuse
deep_dive.abstract: Extracting skills from multi-agent offline dataset improves learning efficiency via sharing task-invariant coordination skills among tasks. In settings where tasks occur sequentially and the space of skills grows exponentially, existing approaches that rely on heuristically designed and fixed-sized skill libraries struggle to resolve the problem of distributional shift and interference, facing catastrophic forgetting and plasticity loss. To address this problem and endow agents with the ability to continually discover and reuse coordination skills in open-environment, we propose COMAD, a principled framework for Continual Offline Multi-agent Skill Discovery via Skill Partition and Reuse. We first discover skills from mixed multi-agent behavior data with an auto-encoder to transform coordination knowledge into reusable coordination skills. Then we construct a skill-augmented policy learning objective with multi-head architectures, explicitly guiding the advantage function with reusable skills identified via a density-based reusability estimator. Theoretical analysis shows our method approximates the optimum of a continual skill discovery problem. Empirical results across diverse MARL benchmarks show that COMAD continually expands its skill library to mitigate interference, achieving superior forward and backward transfer for task streams compared to multiple baselines.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes COMAD for continual offline multi-agent skill discovery, enabling post-training of agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出COMAD框架用于持续离线多智能体技能发现，实现智能体的后训练。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出COMAD框架用于持续离线多智能体技能发现，实现智能体的后训练。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出COMAD框架用于持续离线多智能体技能发现，实现智能体的后训练。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: COMAD addresses catastrophic forgetting in offline multi-agent continual learning by discovering and partitioning skills from mixed data, enabling agents to adapt to new tasks while retaining previous coordination abilities.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对离线多智能体顺序学习任务中出现的灾难性遗忘与可塑性丧失问题，提出了COMAD框架。该框架从混合行为数据中发现并划分可重用的协调技能，通过技能分区与动态扩展，使智能体能够持续适应新任务。实验表明，COMAD在多智能体持续协作场景中有效缓解了遗忘，同时保持了学习新任务的能力，为自主智能体的离线后训练提供了新的持续学习范式。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对离线多智能体顺序学习任务中出现的灾难性遗忘与可塑性丧失问题，提出了COMAD框架。该框架从混合行为数据中发现并划分可重用的协调技能，通过技能分区与动态扩展，使智能体能够持续适应新任务。实验表明，COMAD在多智能体持续协作场景中有效缓解了遗忘，同时保持了学习新任务的能力，为自主智能体的离线后训练提供了新的持续学习范式。
